description: Working on AI and robotics systems for autonomous manipulation and grasping applications.
keyPoints: Developed 6-DoF (Six Degrees of Freedom) pose estimation framework for robotic grasping applications.
Optimized deep learning models and implementation code for use with TensorRT acceleration for real-time inference.

description: Working on autonomous systems and projects for government contracts.
keyPoints: Implemented military drone ATR (Automatic Target Recognition) system with real-time training updates using an imprinting library for on-the-fly labeling.
Developed the Networked Collaborative Autonomous (NCA) specification for the Weapon Open Systems Architecture (WOSA) for the next generation of modular munitions.
Engineered high-performance router software in C for WOSA enabled munitions, creating a low-level reference implementation.

description: Taught AI and Operating Systems courses; engaged in consulting work and community outreach.
keyPoints: Created a new curriculum for hands-on OS development.
Created and modified multiple projects for Network Protocols curriculum.
Led and mentored multiple independent studies courses for deep reinforcement learning, ML weather prediction, and algorithmic trading.
Mentored a capstone research project for deep learning based warehouse automation and monitoring.
Mentored a research project into non-Turing based computational models.
Spearheaded industry consulting projects in AI and performed community outreach via multiple consulting engagements; mentored a team of students in consulting work.

description: Conducted research at the Morphology Evolution & Cognition Lab.
keyPoints: Published multiple peer-reviewed papers on embodied cognition applied to catastrophic forgetting in neural networks.
Presented papers and research at multiple conferences and seminars including the Artificial Life Conference.
Reviewed papers for multiple high-tier journals and conferences in robotics and machine learning (IEEE Robosoft, IROS, RSS, and others).
Mentored graduate and undergraduate researchers in ML and robotics projects, including Master's students in their thesis work.

description: Interned at the Air Force Research Lab.
keyPoints: Applied deep reinforcement learning to drone collaborative/swarm environments.

description: Worked as a research assistant at the Perception Control & Cognition Lab.
keyPoints: Developed a novel method for gradient-based adaptive exploration in deep reinforcement learning.

description: Served as a teaching assistant at Brigham Young University.
keyPoints: Ran and assisted in teaching the Algorithms course.
Guided students in projects and assignments.
Helped in the creation of new projects and assignments.

company: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints
description: Proselytized in the Manaus, Brazil Mission.
keyPoints: Taught various individuals and families the Gospel of Jesus Christ.
Helped individuals and families overcome temporal and spiritual trials and be baptized.
Taught and organized groups of missionaries in proselytizing.
Learned Portuguese and developed interpersonal skills.
